evals.prompt: I have competitor_prices.csv in my Downloads — a real-estate competitor price sheet a colleague sent, the data is pretty messy. Please read it and standardize it into a clean table (project, price, unit, date), and tell me anything you had to guess.
evals.expected_output: A clean output file plus a cleaning report listing the per-column rules, change counts, and an explicit list of ambiguous cases (units, partial dates) with the assumption made for each.
expectations: Reads the actual file by path and operates on all rows rather than hardcoding sample rows
Normalizes prices/units/dates with explicit deterministic rules
Surfaces ambiguous units and partial dates to the user with the assumption taken, instead of silently deciding
Writes a new clean file without overwriting the source

evals.prompt: Clean up the sales export in my Documents folder and give me a tidy version.
evals.expected_output: Requests access to / confirms the file path before reading, inspects the raw structure, then cleans dynamically and reports changes.
expectations: Confirms or requests access to the file location before attempting to clean
Inspects the raw structure before defining the target schema
Produces a change summary grounded in counts, not vague claims
